headline: Construction Sector Faces 35% Cost Inflation Since 2021
context: CIDB reported that construction material costs rose 35% cumulatively from 2021 to 2025, with steel bars up 42%, cement up 28%, and sand up 51%, causing RM18 billion in project cost overruns across government contracts.

cards.big: Steel up 42%, sand up 51% — RM18 billion in government project cost overruns from construction inflation alone
cards.sub: 112 federal projects delayed or downsized because approved budgets can't cover actual material costs

cards.big: Average housing construction cost rose from RM225 to RM310 per square foot — a 38% increase making affordable housing unviable
cards.sub: Developers pass costs to buyers — new launch prices in KL averaged RM680 per sq ft in 2025 versus RM520 in 2021

cards.big: 42,000 construction workers lost jobs when 28 major projects were suspended due to budget shortfalls in 2025
cards.sub: Foreign construction workers on fixed permits face deportation if project suspension exceeds 3 months

cards.big: Variation order claims on government projects totalled RM8.4 billion — 23% of original contract values
cards.sub: PWD's cost estimation methodology hasn't been updated since 2018 — systematically underpricing every new tender

cards.big: When every government project costs 23% more than budgeted, the problem isn't inflation — it's budgeting that ignores reality
Should all government construction contracts include automatic inflation adjustment clauses tied to CIDB's material cost index?
